programme.title: Boda gay
programme.desc: Lily mejora y le pide perdón a Arriaga y a Victoria. Stefano y Jean Marie se van a casar y Odette apoya a su hijo. Lily le desea felicidad a Guzmán y Nikki y terminan amigos. Aníbal hace negocios cochinos.

programme.title: Dolor de padre
programme.desc: Día de boda de candelaria y Aníbal. Nelson no invitado llega con un regalo de bodas. Todas las verdades salen. Salcero quiere confesar sus delitos. Arriaga sale de la iglesia y sufre un shock.

programme.title: La advertencia de Salsero
programme.desc: Guzmán y Nikki encuentran a Arriaga. Arriaga no puede ver. Aníbal escapa en un jet. Candelaria se entrega a la ley. Salcero se confiesa con Arriaga. Kendra infecta a Nelson.

programme.title: Gran final: Part 1
programme.desc: Nelson sufre un accidente mortal. Aníbal llega a NY. Kendra va al velorio. Nelson deja sus acciones A Victoria. Nace su hijo y Arriaga recupera la vista. Aníbal en peligro de muerte. Salcero, Guzmán y Arriaga planean atrapar a Kendra.

programme.title: Season 1
programme.desc: The pilot outline for "Amores Verdaderos" "Amores Verdaderos pilot introduces Victoria, a successful woman engaged to Armando. She meets José Ángel, a humble gardener, leading to unexpected connections and family conflicts."

programme.title: Gran final: Part 3
programme.desc: Nikki Guzmán Arriaga y Victoria se casan al mismo tiempo. Roy pide la mano de Liliana. Aníbal es dado 82 años de cárcel por sus delitos. Salcero sale libre y se reconcilia con guillo y Beatriz.

programme.title: Amores verdaderos
programme.desc: José Ángel saves Victoria from a kidnapping attempt which puts their lives in danger. Nelson is shocked to meet Kendra.

programme.title: Propuesta de trabajo
programme.desc: Salsero and Kendra make a plan so that Victoria thinks that José Ángel is an excellent bodyguard and hires him. Surprisingly, Victoria asks Arriaga to work for her as her bodyguard.

programme.title: En prisión
programme.desc: José Ángel decides to resign so that Victoria has a better bodyguard. José Ángel and Guzmán face some thieves, but things don't go as they expected.

programme.title: ¡Voy a ser papá!
programme.desc: Silvia le confiesa a Luis que está embarazada y él comienza a comprar muchas cosas para el bebé. Magdalena y su familia se vuelven ricos y comienzan a derrochar su dinero.

programme.title: La casa de las Lomas
programme.desc: Magdalena les dice a los vecinos que ella y su familia se mudarán a Las Lomas y le pide a Luis comprarle una mansión.

programme.title: Perdimos todo
programme.desc: Luis comienza a molestar a Silvia todo el tiempo queriendo controlar lo que hace para cuidar del bebé. Magdalena invierte todo su dinero en una empresa fantasma y pierde todo.

programme.title: Luisito, corazón de plomo
programme.desc: Magda le dice a Luis que no pueden mudarse a su mansión y le pide de vuelta las llaves del departamento, pero él se niega a dejarlos vivir en en edificio otra vez y todos los vecinos tratan de convencerlo de darles otra oportunidad.

programme.title: El muñeco vudú
programme.desc: Germán y Jorjáis encuentran un muñeco vudú, asumen que alguien está haciendo brujería en el edificio. Pedro, Cris y Vanesa tienen problemas con el WiFi de su departamento.

programme.title: Síntomas empáticos
programme.desc: Luis descubre que es él (y no Silvia), quien tiene los síntomas del embarazo. Los vecinos encuentran en la calle carteles pegados de: "Se busca a Jorjáis".

programme.title: La hija de Jorjais
programme.desc: Una mujer le pregunta a Germán sobre el paradero de Jorjáis, y él se sorprende al descubrir que ella es su hija. Vanessa cree que tiene la maldición de la soltería.

programme.title: Niño o niña
programme.desc: Luis ya no sabe que hacer con Silvia porque tiene muchos cambios de humor por el embarazo, pero sus vecinos lo convencen de darle una sorpresa que jamás olvidará.

programme.title: Conserje aprendiz
programme.desc: Lorena acepta hacer un casting para interpretar el papel de La Doña. Germán decide enseñarle a Borrego a ser un gran conserje.

programme.title: El perfume
programme.desc: Magdalena se aprovecha de las náuseas que siente Silvia por el embarazo para que Luis le compre todo lo que ella quiere.

programme.title: Apolonio y la víbora
programme.desc: Vanesa lleva una serpiente al edificio y se le escapa. Ahora tiene que recuperarla sin que los vecinos se enteren. Silvia y Luis toman un curso psicoprofiláctico con una chica que resulta ser la ex de Luis.

programme.title: La chapa
programme.desc: Luis descompone la chapa de la reja, quedando él y Silvia afuera y el resto de los vecinos adentro. En lo que reparan la chapa, los vecinos usan a Luis de mandadero.

programme.title: Los tocatimbres
programme.desc: Germán cree que los timbres del edificio están embrujados, pues alguien los hace sonar constantemente. Luis intenta colocar unas repisas en el cuarto del bebé.

programme.title: Arturo más pordiosero
programme.desc: Los López Pérez se hacen pasar por pordioseros para recibir caridad. Pedro lleva varios días sin bañarse y Cris le exige que lo haga.

programme.title: El parto
programme.desc: Silvia comienza el trabajo de parto en el departamento, no hay tiempo para llegar al hospital. Luis recibirá al bebé con la ayuda de los vecinos.

programme.title: Vecinos consejeros
programme.desc: El hijo de Silvia y Luis no para de llorar y ya no saben qué hacer. Los vecinos les aconsejan cómo cuidar al bebé mientras Germán improvisa un gimnasio en el vestíbulo.

programme.title: Mucha luz
programme.desc: Hay fallas de energía eléctrica en algunos de los departamentos y Luis tiene que ir a trabajar al departamento de los López Pérez. Germán se prepara para su examen de admisión a la preparatoria en línea.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Northern Rockies: British Columbia Grizzly Part 1
programme.desc: Steve and his buddy Ryan Callaghan, a British Columbia guide, hike into the steep backcountry of northern British Columbia in search of grizzlies and black bears. Among the beutiful alpine vista, this adventure includes some intense and up-close bear action ‚Äì almost too close.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Northern Rockies: British Columbia Grizzly Part 2
programme.desc: Steve and his buddy Ryan Callaghan, a British Columbia guide, hike into the steep backcountry of northern British Columbia in search of grizzlies and black bears. Among the beutiful alpine vista, this adventure includes some intense and up-close bear action ‚Äì almost too close.

programme.title: MeatEater - Opening Day: Wisconsin Whitetail Deer Part 1
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his buddy Doug Duren give comedians Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen their first taste.

programme.title: MeatEater - Opening Day: Wisconsin Whitetail Deer Part 2
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his buddy Doug Duren hunt with Joe Rogan.

programme.title: MeatEater - Spring Break: Florida Osceola Turkey
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his friend and wild turkey specialist, Robert Abernathy, head to southern Florida to hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater - Boone and Bugles: Kentucky Bull Elk
programme.desc: Steven Rinella is invited by the Rocky Mountain Elk Foundation for a special opportunity to hunt elk during the rut in the mountainous coal country of southeastern Kentucky, a place where his hero Daniel Boone once roamed. This is Steve's first eastern elk hunt, and he quickly realizes that the rules of the game here are different. If he manages to track one down, he'll prepare what was supposedly Boone's favorite meal: elk liver.

programme.title: MeatEater - Cold Water: Alaskan Seafood Cooking Special
programme.desc: Join Steve on this Cooking Special as he dives into the best of Alaskan seafood, featuring shrimp, crab, fish, and clams. Learn how to make easy, delicious seafood recipes that showcase the fresh flavors of the sea. Whether you're a seasoned cook or a beginner, Steve's simple steps will help you prepare mouthwatering dishes that capture the essence of Alaskan waters.

programme.title: MeatEater - Big Buck Dreams: Colorado Mule Deer
programme.desc: Steve's annual quest for a giant mule deer buck continues this year in southwest Colorado. Here, Steve fills us in on his favorite critter, the mule deer. As he hunts will share his vast knowledge about these special western game animals including how to hunt, butcher, and most importantly how cook them-if he can put his tag on one.

programme.title: MeatEater - Surf N Turk: Wisconsin Turkey and Carp
programme.desc: Steven Rinella scratches his spring turkey itch this year as he visits Southeast Wisconsin to call in some big eastern Toms with his buddy Jerod Fink. The guys also plan to do some bow fishing after dark so they can enjoy a real Midwest turkey and fish fry.

programme.title: MeatEater - Southeast Alaska Blacktail Deer
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and Evan Hafer head into Southeast Alaska's coastal rainforest to hunt Sitka black-tailed deer.

programme.title: MeatEater - Nebraska Whitetails
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and Clay Newcomb head to Nebraska's Sandhills for a whitetail deer hunt, guided by Jordan.

programme.title: MeatEater - Louisiana Ducks
programme.desc: Steven Rinella joins Jean-Paul Bourgeois and Ronnie Collins in Louisiana‚ Mississippi Delta for a duck hunt. Surrounded by the unique landscape of the Delta, they target multiple duck species. After a successful hunt, the team returns to camp, where Jean-Paul prepares a traditional Cajun meal featuring the day‚ catch. Experience the cultural tradition of turning fresh game into flavorful, local dishes like duck gumbo and crawfish boil.

programme.title: MeatEater - New Mexico Turkey
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads to New Mexico's Gila Wilderness with his friend Carl Malcolm to hunt Merriam's turkeys. Set against a backdrop of stunning landscapes and rich history, they explore the first federally protected wilderness in the world. Although few and far between, the duo find gobbling birds through hard scouting and perserverence.

programme.title: What a Day (Can Blanche Do What Bondi Couldn't?)
programme.desc:
President Donald Trump officially nominated Todd Blanche to serve as Attorney General. After firing Pam Bondi back in April, Trump went with his former defense attorney to lead more than 10,000 attorneys. And it seems pretty clear why: Trump wants someone to do exactly what he wants. Like prosecute his political enemies or set up a 1.776 billion dollar MAGA "anti-weaponization" fund. But by doing Trump's bidding, Blanche has made many of his own enemies. So what does that mean for Trump's legal agenda? To find out, we spoke with Ken White. He's a former federal prosecutor, partner at Brown White & Osborn LLP, and co-host of the podcast Serious Trouble.
And in headlines, Trump blames Iran for shooting down a U.S. Army helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, a new study on alcohol confirms our worst fears, and a federal judge strikes down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas.

programme.title: Keep It (P!nk Tony Club with Wendi McLendon-Covey, Chris Schleicher and Rheeqrheeq Chainey)
programme.desc: Louis Virtel is joined by TV writers Chris Schleicher and Rheeqrheeq Chainey to gab about the Kylie Minogue Netflix documentary, Madonna’s new short film, and other projects they’re watching right now. Then they discuss the highs and lows of Broadway’s biggest night: The Tony Awards. Plus, comedy powerhouse Wendi McLendon-Covey comes by to talk about her show “St. Denis Medical.”

programme.title: Assembly Required with Stacey Abrams (Why Is Disability Still an Afterthought?)
programme.desc: What does disability rights and representation really look like? While the Trump administration continues to decimate access to healthcare and dismantle civil rights, disabled Americans are often among those most affected and most overlooked. More than 70 million adults in the United States live with a disability, navigating barriers that extend far beyond health care. This week, Stacey is joined by Marlene Sallo, Executive Director of the National Disability Rights Network, and disability rights advocate and motivational speaker Spencer West. They discuss the challenges facing the disability community, what many people get wrong about disability rights, and why building a more accessible and inclusive society is a social justice issue that impacts us all.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (What Actually Happens in ICE Detention)
programme.desc: A new chapter of ICE terror has begun, but instead of taking place on the street—where agents are held accountable by protesters and their phones–—mistreatment and coercion is now happening behind the closed doors of ICE detention facilities. These private prisons are operated by a company with close ties to the Trump administration, and have such abominable conditions that detainees at several facilities are waging hunger strikes. To get an idea of the mistreatment immigrants are facing, Alex speaks with Melissa Shepard, an attorney with Immigration Defenders Law Center, whose clients are given dirty water and spoiled food, retaliated against for participating in hunger strikes, and endlessly pressured to self deport. Then, she puts it all into context with Caitlin Dickerson, an immigration reporter at The Atlantic who recently embedded in Honduras to interview recent deportees from the U.S.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (Election Chaos: Texas Republicans vs California Dems)
programme.desc: Could reality tv star Spencer Pratt become Mayor of Los Angeles? And could Trump’s endorsement of Ken Paxton cause Texas to go blue? This week, Alex dives into all things primaries to see if we can read the tea leaves for what awaits both Republicans and Democrats in the future. She’s joined by Nikki Laurenzo, anchor and political reporter for FOX40 in Sacramento, to break down the chaotic and still very undecided races shaping California politics. Then she speaks to Crooked Media’s Dan Pfeifffer, co-host of Pod Save America and author of the Message Box newsletter, to get a read on the consequences of Senator John Cornyn’s loss to Ken Paxton, and why a crowded gubernatorial primary in California could be a warning for Democratic presidential hopefuls in 2028.

programme.title: Pod Save America (Gavin Newsom Is Finally Comfortable with Himself)
programme.desc: California Governor Gavin Newsom sits down with Jon and Tommy before a live audience in Los Angeles to discuss Trump's war on Iran, the crowded California gubernatorial primary, and his new book Young Man in a Hurry: A Memoir of Discovery. The governor talks about his close childhood friendship with the Getty family, issuing the first same-sex marriage licenses nationwide as San Francisco Mayor, and his surprising childhood pet, Potter the otter.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (The Other MAGA War….At Home)
programme.desc: While the Trump administration wages war against Iran and broadens their military scope in the northern hemisphere, there are more subtle campaigns being pushed here at home – like the one to convince women to do nothing more than have babies and stay at home. This week, Alex speaks to former trad wife and host of “The Wake Her Up Podcast”, Sharon Johnson, about her upbringing in the Mormon Church, what it was like to discover she could have agency and choice in her late thirties, and how she feels about trad wives trending. Then Alex is joined by host of Crooked’s “Hysteria” podcast, Erin Ryan, to talk about the broader political goals behind the Heritage Foundation’s Project 2026 plan, and how it attempts to punish women who don’t fit their ideals.

programme.desc: Seeking answers, Scott (Tyler Posey). Allison (Crystal Reed) and Stiles (Dylan O'Brien) hear stories told by two unlikely narrators -- Peter (Ian Bohen) and Gerard (Michael Hogan) -- and discover a secret about the color of a werewolf's eyes.

programme.title: The Girl Who Knew Too Much
programme.desc: As Allison (Crystal Reed) begins to suspect someone close to her may be involved in the murders, Scott (Tyler Posey) and Stiles (Dylan O'Brien) conclude that Lydia's (Holland Roden) rising talents may be their best bet to solving the murders.

programme.title: The Overlooked
programme.desc: Trapped inside an evacuated Beacon Hills hospital during a powerful storm, Scott (Tyler Posey) and Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) must defend themselves against the Alphas while trying to figure out how to save Cora's (Adelaide Kane) life.

programme.title: Alpha Pact
programme.desc: With the threat growing more personal and time running out, Scott (Tyler Posey) is forced to make a difficult choice to save family and friends while, at the same time, Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) and Peter (Ian Bohen) try to figure out how to save Cora.

programme.title: Lunar Ellipse
programme.desc: In the mid-season finale, under Deaton's advice, Scott (Tyler Posey) and his friends use an ancient but dangerous ritual to help them both save the lives of the people they love and stop Deucalion (Gideon Emery) from achieving his sinister goal.

programme.title: Anchors
programme.desc: Weeks after saving the lives of their parents, Scott, Stiles and Allison suffer strange side effects from their ritual sacrifice. At the same time, the newly enlightened Sheriff Stilinski scours through old cases, looking for supernatural causes.

programme.title: More Bad Than Good
programme.desc: Scott (Tyler Posey) and his friends race to figure out how to catch the Werecoyote Malia before it's hunted down and killed by her father. Meanwhile, Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) and Peter (Ian Bohen) are broken out of captivity by an unlikely ally.

programme.title: Galvanize
programme.desc: On the night before Halloween, Mischief Night, a convicted murderer brought to Beacon Hills Hospital for surgery escapes, forcing Scott (Tyler Posey) and his friends to step in to help when his unexpected target becomes clear.

programme.title: Lisa's Jam Session
programme.desc: Oliver finds himself in a jam after asking Lisa to make him homemade fruit preserves. An episode deliciously devoted to Lisa's culinary escapades, Lisa musters all of her Hungarian might to make Oliver the best peach jam he's ever tasted.

programme.title: Oliver Vs The Phone Company
programme.desc: After years of substandard service, Oliver is hellbent on throwing the book at the Hooterville Phone Company. When it becomes clear that the local utility oversight board can't do much about it, he decides to handle matters directly.

programme.title: Oliver Takes Over The Phone Company
programme.desc: Mr. Trendell, the president of the Hooterville Phone Company, hands the company over to a delighted Oliver. But after a day of crossed wires and a switchboard repairman with some lose connections of his own, Oliver is ready to call it quits.

programme.title: Don't Count Your Tomatoes Before They're Picked
programme.desc: For the first time, Oliver has a successful crop that needs picking. His tomatoes have grown beautifully. But matters at the phone company continue divide his precious time, so Oliver looks for an interested buyer to take the company off his hands.

programme.title: A Kind Word For The President
programme.desc: Being president of the Hooterville Phone Company has been a rude awakening for Oliver. The switchboard operator is now calling him at five in the morning! After a bevy of new complaints from unhappy customers, Oliver has just about had enough.

programme.title: Eb Elopes
programme.desc: One might think that when Eb takes a vacation things around the farm might go more smoothly. But when Eb elopes, leaving his cousin Walter to take his place, it turns out to be a horrible idea. Walter barely knows a weed from a hole in the ground!

programme.title: The Thing
programme.desc: Lisa is reminded that she stored a birthday present for Oliver years ago: a genuine Stavinsky. But what is it? It's a modern art sculpture that is two parts plumbing pipes, one part tire chains, and one part birdbath. Oliver calls it "The Thing."

programme.title: Das Lumpen
programme.desc: The continually evolving story of how Lisa (Eva Gabor) and Oliver (Oliver) fell in love gets yet another version in this delightful episode. But one thing is for sure: Lisa and Oliver are just as in love today as the day when he fell out of the sky.

programme.title: Jealousy, English Style
programme.desc: Green Acres is greener than ever, with jealousy. When Oliver (Eddie Albert) hires a handsome, young, intelligent Brit to take over his farm work while he is away, it doesn't take long for the newcomer to make a strong impression on Lisa (Eva Gabor).

programme.title: Haney's New Image
programme.desc: Mr. Haney is up to his money-making schemes again. When a government representative tells Mr. Haney that the state is looking to purchase the Douglas farm for a new highway, Mr. Haney arranges to buy back the farm from the Douglases at a steal.

programme.title: Alf And Ralph Break Up
programme.desc: Ralph, the lady carpenter, runs away from home and stays with the Douglases after having a fight with her brother, Alf. While Oliver may never get his bedroom finished, he gains a maid as Ralph takes over as the Douglases' live-in housekeeper.

programme.title: No Trespassing
programme.desc: Picking apples while on a picnic, Oliver runs into the feisty Ira Hatch, the owner of the apple orchard. However, once back in town, Mr. Drucker tells Oliver that Hatch has been dead for years. An incredulous Oliver agrees to see a psychologist.

programme.title: Eb Returns
programme.desc: After his elopement and cross-country honeymoon, Eb returns to Hooterville. But his new wife is nowhere to be found! After sending postcards from all across the country, Eb has to confess that his fianc� stood him up at the alter on their big day.

programme.title: Not Guilty
programme.desc: Someone has stolen $300 from Mr. Drucker's general store, and it looks like Eb is the culprit. How else could he have bought himself a new car that cost, you guessed it, exactly $300? But that is just the first twist of the many turns of this story.

programme.title: How To Succeed In TV Without Really Trying
programme.desc: Lisa awakes, saying she's dreamt that she must invest in copper. When Oliver turns that idea down, she seizes on the opportunity to invest in a ten-year-old genius's electric company. Twenty-eight dollars buys her a third of the business.

programme.title: Arnold, Boy Hero
programme.desc: Arnold, the Ziffels' television-loving pig, has taken on a paper route to save enough money for a new color set. But when bank robbers show up and threaten his dreams, and Lisa and Oliver are held captive in their own home, Arnold has to fight back.

programme.title: Flight To Nowhere
programme.desc: The farmers of Hooterville are headed to France! Or at least they are if they can get organized. As part of an agricultural exchange program, the farmers are offered a chance to join a chartered government flight to Europe.

programme.title: Reflections
programme.desc: A bright day and still water unite.

programme.desc: A seascape with an approaching storm.

programme.title: Morticia, The Sculptress
programme.desc: In need of artistic fulfillment, Morticia takes up sculpting. An art critic views her first endeavor--a three-eyed Gomez statue--and clubs it with his cane. Gomez, who won't have Morticia's heart broken gets, a seedy art dealer to buy it.

programme.title: Gomez, The Reluctant Lover
programme.desc: Pugsley copies one of his dad's old love letters and sends it to his teacher. Miss Dunbar comes to visit the Addams home and confesses to Gomez that no man has ever fallen so in love with her, leading Morticia to pretend to be the jealous wife.

programme.title: Feud In The Addams Family
programme.desc: After Wednesday befriends a new boy her age, Robespierre Courtney, Morticia invites his parents, Mr. and Mrs. Henry Courtney, to visit the Addams house for tea. Unbeknownst to them, the Courtney's are hoping to meet a number of the social set.

programme.title: Gomez, The Cat Burglar
programme.desc: There is a neighborhood burglar on the loose, and Morticia has reason to believe it could be Gomez. Morticia decides to probe his subconscious mind for a key to his criminality after she and Uncle Fester discover loot hidden in the storeroom.

programme.title: Portrait Of Gomez
programme.desc: Morticia's pet plant Cleopatra gobbles up her favorite photo of Gomez from the family album. When the original photographer is found and asked to make a duplicate picture, it is discovered that he is now with the Department of Motor Vehicles.

programme.title: Morticia's Dilemma
programme.desc: Gomez plays host to Don Xavier Molines, a family friend during his childhood in Spain, and his daughter, Consuella. Morticia's hospitality is put to the test when Consuella reveals that she has been promised in marriage to Gomez since childhood.

programme.title: Christmas With The Addams Family
programme.desc: When Wednesday's and Pugsley's faith in Santa Claus is shaken, Uncle Fester is chosen to back up Santa in case the real St. Nick is too busy to come. However, Fester-Santa gets stuck in the chimney, so each of the other adults fill in for him.

programme.title: Uncle Fester, Tycoon
programme.desc: Fester is so smitten upon receipt of a photo of his love, a bearded carnival lady, that he borrows stamp money to mail her a proposal. Morticia dons a beard and poses as the mother to convince Fester that he will have to work to support a wife.

programme.title: Morticia & Gomez Vs. Fester & Grandmama
programme.desc: Because she thinks Fester (Jackie Coogan) and Grandmama (Blossom Rock) are spoiling the children, Morticia (Carolyn Jones) hires a governess. Insulted, Fester paints a white line down the center of the Addams house, dividing it in half.

programme.title: Fester Goes On A Diet
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez see Fester conferring with TV muscleman Jack LaGrann (Jack LaLanne), and think that Fester is planning a television career. However, Uncle Fester confides to Lurch that he has to go on a secret diet and exercise.

programme.title: The Great Treasure Hunt
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez find Grandfather Pegleg's treasure map and summon Captain Grimby. They hope to charter his boat for the treasure hunt. But after meeting the Captain, Morticia is careful to keep the nature of the mission secret.

programme.title: Ophelia Finds Romance
programme.desc: Ophelia Frump is madly in love with Horatio Bartholomew, the man who has everything, except the truth about her relatives. Morticia, not convinced by Bartholomew, persuades playboy Cousin Itt to stir the embers of his past romance with Ophelia.

programme.title: Pugsley's Allowance
programme.desc: Pugsley shocks his parents when he gives up his new atomic reactor as kid stuff and announces that he wants to find his own job. His first try is as a medical assistant to Dr. Bird. He turns up, scalpel in hand, ready to help during an operation.

programme.title: Happy Birthday, Grandma Frump
programme.desc: Grandma Frump (guest star Margaret Hamilton) comes to the mistaken conclusion that her daughter, Morticia (Carolyn Jones), and Gomez (John Astin) plan to pack her off to an old folks home. Actually, they plan to send her to a beauty farm.

programme.title: Morticia, The Decorator
programme.desc: Morticia, in order to be part of the National Beautification Program, decides to become a home decorator and offers her services to the neighbors. Next door neighbor, and insurance agent, Joe Digby hires Morticia in hopes of selling them insurance.

programme.title: How To Overcome The Worst Pain Of Your Life
programme.desc: From Hollywood’s biggest star to a near-tragic ending, Jeremy Renner’s comeback is a story of pain, recovery, and incredible resilience. How did the Oscar nominee fight to bounce back, and what’s next now that he’s fully recovered? Expect to learn the real story of what happened to Jeremy during the snowplow accident, what it feels like to get run over and see your eyeball fall out of it’s socket, how Jeremy was able to recover so well and look as just as he did before the accident, how to deal with excruciating amount of pain, how Jeremy’s meditation practices came in handy during his darkest hours, what it was like being a part of The Avengers & the craziness of being one of the biggest stars in the world, and much more…

programme.title: 41 Harsh Truths Nobody Wants To Admit
programme.desc: Alex’s Twitter has been one of my favourite sources of insights over the last few years. Today we get to go through some of his best lessons about life, human behaviour, psychology, business and resilience again. And as always this is so, so good. Expect to learn the skills needed to thrive in todays every changing world, why a few bad days shouldn’t ruin the rest of your year, how Alex’s mind has changed about work and happiness in the past year, how to figure out what you want in life, the rare dynamic between Alex and his wife Leila, why having a work life balance isn’t all it’s cracked up to be, why more money won’t buy you more happiness, how to get a top tier girls and much more…

programme.title: The Human Operators
programme.desc: Like other Star Fleet vessels, Ship (voice of Malcolm McDowell) has evolved and now controls the only human (Jack Noseworthy) aboard. But the relationship takes a dramatic turn when Man meets Woman and fights machine in a desperate bid for freedom!

programme.title: Blank Slate
programme.desc: Do our memories define our identity? If we are merely the sum of our experiences, what do we become if those experiences are lost? Hope Wilson (Robbie Chong) faces this question when she meets a man who has purposely erased his own memories.

programme.title: What Will The Neighbors Think?
programme.desc: Courtesy of a freak accident (and The Outer Limits), an agoraphobe (Jane Adams) begins to hear the private thoughts of all of the tenants in her building...the good, the bad and the steamy. And it seems at least one of them may want to kill her.

programme.title: The Shroud
programme.desc: A scientist -- who is also a religious zealot (David Ogden Stiers) -- secretly implants an embryo created with DNA from the Shroud of Turin into a young woman (Samantha Mathis) in order to scientifically engineer the Second Coming of Christ.

programme.title: Ripper
programme.desc: The Outer Limits come to Victorian Whitechapel when a London doctor (Cary Elwes) races to capture the alien creature responsible for the Jack the Ripper murders. But when the alien frames the good doctor for the murders, his time may be up.

programme.title: Tribunal
programme.desc: The son (Saul Rubinek) of a concentration camp survivor (Peter Boretski) gets some unique help in proving the guilt of a former Nazi (Jan Rubes) living in the U.S....courtesy of a time traveler (Alex Daikun) and, of course, The Outer Limits.

programme.title: Summit
programme.desc: The planets Earth and Dregocia struggle to re-establish a fragile trust after a summit goes tragically wrong. Can a diplomat (Marcia Cross) calm the hawkish nature of both species…and can she overcome her own prejudices to save both worlds?

programme.title: Descent
programme.desc: A meek anthropologist (Leland Orser) develops a serum for genetic dominance and tests it on himself. Now his newfound confidence takes complete control of him, turning him into an irrational, overly aggressive troglodyte! Can he reverse the process?

programme.title: The Haven
programme.desc: The Haven is a high-tech apartment building which is run by supercomputer and almost completely eliminates the need for human contact. But when things begin to go wrong, neighbors who have never met must learn to work together in order to survive!
